title: Fluency Practice Guide
definition: Fluency comes from consistent daily speaking practice, not just grammar study. Use these techniques to build confidence and natural rhythm in spoken English.
rules: Shadowing: Play a short English audio/video clip, pause every sentence, and repeat it aloud trying to match the speaker's rhythm and intonation.
Picture description: Look at any photo and describe it out loud for 1-2 minutes - who, what, where, when, why.
Story reading aloud: Read a short story or news article aloud daily for 5-10 minutes to build pronunciation and pacing.
Self-talk: Narrate your own actions in English throughout the day ('I am making tea now, then I will reply to emails').
Debate practice: Pick a simple topic and speak for 1 minute for it, then 1 minute against it, to build quick thinking in English.
Group discussion: Practice with a friend or language partner on a topic, taking turns to speak and respond.
Public speaking: Record yourself speaking for 2 minutes on a familiar topic, then listen back and note filler words (um, like) to reduce.
tips: Consistency matters more than duration — 15 minutes daily beats 2 hours once a week.
Don't aim for perfect grammar while speaking; focus on getting your message across, then review mistakes afterward.
Record your voice weekly and compare progress over a month — you'll notice real improvement.
Use the app's Speak buttons on example sentences throughout the lessons to hear correct pronunciation and repeat after them.
examples: 30-Day Plan - Week 1: Shadow 1 short video daily + review 10 vocabulary words.
30-Day Plan - Week 2: Add picture description practice (2 minutes daily).
30-Day Plan - Week 3: Add debate practice on simple topics (e.g. 'Is social media good for society?').
30-Day Plan - Week 4: Record a 2-minute talk on a topic you know well and self-review it.

quiz.question: What is 'shadowing' in speaking practice?
quiz.explanation: Shadowing means repeating what you hear aloud to build natural rhythm.

quiz.question: Which matters most for building fluency?
quiz.explanation: Regular short practice sessions build fluency faster than occasional long ones.
